Hyderabad gets monsoon-like relief after weeks of intense heatwave
Hyderabad experienced a welcome break from an intense heatwave as overnight showers, thunder and gusty winds brought monsoon-like conditions to the city. Moderate rainfall, recorded at several locations, provided much-needed relief to residents after weeks of scorching temperatures.
Hyderabad: After weeks of enduring a relentless and scorching heatwave that pushed maximum temperatures past 42 degrees Celsius and even 43 degrees Celsius at some places, people in Hyderabad finally received a welcome reprieve, when they woke-up on Wednesday morning.
It was a rare pleasant chill, reminiscent of the monsoon season, with cloudy skies lingering over the city, painting a stark contrast to the harsh, sun-drenched mornings of the past several weeks.
People, many of whom had been struggling with the oppressive heatwave, took to social media to celebrate the refreshing climate.
While the TSDPS recorded moderate rainfall, with Malkajgiri leading at 2.4 cm, the true impact was the sigh of relief among residents across multiple neighborhoods.
Weatherwise, till Tuesday midnight, nothing had changed in Hyderabad. However, shortly after that, dark clouds, accompanied by thunder, lightning and gusty winds, started rolling-in, signalling a dramatic shift in the weather conditions.
Steady, cooling showers along with gusty winds persisted throughout the night, bathing the city in a gentle downpour. And by Wednesday morning, the weather had transformed.
For a city that had remained dry for weeks, the overnight showers were the season’s first meaningful showers, which brought much needed relief and calmness to the general public.

Hyderabad, may 27: Residents of Hyderabad woke up to cloudy skies, cool winds and light rain on Wednesday after weeks of intense heatwave conditions across the city.
The sudden weather change brought major relief to people who had been facing temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius in several parts of Hyderabad over the past few weeks.
According to the Telangana State Development Planning Society, moderate rainfall was recorded in parts of the city, with Malkajgiri receiving around 2.4 cm rainfall.

full_text:
Boothpur in Mahbubnagar registered the highest rainfall of 112 mm
Hyderabad: The sudden rain in several parts of the State including Hyderabad has brought much needed relief to people from the sweltering heat conditions since Tuesday night. Boothpur in Mahbubnagar registered the highest rainfall of 112 mm.
The rain continued till Wednesday morning. The day temperatures also dipped significantly in several districts.
The drastic dip in temperature enabled the people to switch off their air coolers and air-conditioners since morning and enjoyed cool weather. Some netizens shared selfies taken from their smart phones from the terrace and balconies to their friends and relatives explaining how weather changed completely in Hyderabad last night.
Hyderabad recorded a temperature of 42.2 degrees Celsius at 8.30 am on Tuesday but it is 23 degrees Celsius at 8.30 am on Wednesday dropping temperature to double digit, according to India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Similarly, Khammam, which registered the highest temperature of 45.4 degrees Celsius at 8.30 am on Tuesday, recorded 36.4 degrees Celsius at 8.30 on Wednesday. The day temperature registered a dip of nine degrees Celsius in Khammam since last night.
Ramagundam, which registered the second highest day temperature on Tuesday, registered a dip in temperature on Wednesday. It recorded 45.2 degrees Celsius at 8.30 am on Tuesday and 28 degrees Celsius on Wednesday at 8.30 am.
